summary: Joel Spolsky announces the launch of a job board specifically for positions in India, hosted at jobs.joelonsoftware.co.in. He prices job listings at $50 (about 2200 rupees) as an introductory rate to help fill the board. He differentiates his board from major Indian job sites like Monsterindia and Naukri.com by promising fewer but higher-quality applicants, with a money-back guarantee.
key_points: Launch of a region-specific job board targeting the Indian market
Introductory pricing of $50 per listing to attract early adopters
Value proposition centered on quality over quantity of applicants, differentiating from mass-market job boards
money_quotes: Unlike the huge boards, Monsterindia, Naukri.com, etc., a job posted to Joel on Software will get far fewer resumes, but they will be of much higher quality.
key_insight: A niche job board targeting a self-selected audience of engaged readers can deliver higher-quality candidates than mass-market alternatives, even in a large market like India.
